channel.title: 2026-03-19 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-19 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: [ASAP] PNcsp+: A Periodic Number-Based Crystal Structure Prediction Method Enhanced by Machine Learning
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Cem Oran, Riccarda Caputo, Pierre Villars, and Adem Tekin
Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation DOI: 10.1021/acs.jctc.6c00044

item.title: Framework to completely bypass expensive DFT calculations via graph neural networks for vacancy formation energy predictions in FCC high entropy alloys
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Nathan Linton, Parampreet Singh, Dilpuneet S. Aidhy
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02037-6 Framework to completely bypass expensive DFT calculations via graph neural networks for vacancy formation energy predictions in FCC high entropy alloys

Nano Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.nanolett.6c00270

item.title: [ASAP] Reversal of Spin Signals in Anthraquinone-Based Magnetic Tunnel Junctions
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 L. Chen, J. Perrin, I. Arbouch, B. Dlubak, P. Seneor, R. Mattana, F. Godel, K. Sobnath, G. Wang, B. Janvier, P. Filloux, F. Mallet, C. Barraud, J. Cornil, C. Van Dyck, and P. Martin
Nano Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.nanolett.5c05222

item.title: Discovery of a novel half metallic 2D Cr 2 Se 3 monolayer with high Curie temperature from correlated antiferromagnetic 2D CrSe 2
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Khaled Badawy, Lianxi Zheng, Nirpendra Singh
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02029-6 Discovery of a novel half metallic 2D Cr 2 Se 3 monolayer with high Curie temperature from correlated antiferromagnetic 2D CrSe 2

item.title: Electron Delocalization Induced by 3d–4f Orbital Hybridization of Co─Ce Atom Pairs Toward Enhanced Electromagnetic Wave Attenuation and Antibacterial Performance
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Science: Table of Contents
👤 Da Li, Yinyin Zhao, Yang Zhang, Cui Ni, Xiaoguang Zhao, Chongchang Zhou, Guoqiang Ren, Pengfei Liu, Xiubo Xie
Advanced Science, EarlyView.

item.title: Enhancement of quantum annealing via n-local catalysts
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Roopayan Ghosh, Luca A. Nutricati, Natasha Feinstein, P. A. Warburton, and Sougato Bose
Author(s): Roopayan Ghosh, Luca A. Nutricati, Natasha Feinstein, P. A. Warburton, and Sougato Bose The potential quantum speedup in solving optimization problems via adiabatic quantum annealing is often hindered by the closing of the energy gap during the anneal, especially when this gap scales exponentially with system size. In this work, we alleviate this bottleneck by demonstrating that for th… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 013301] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Field theory of linear spin waves in finite textured ferromagnets
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 T. Valet, K. Yamamoto, B. Pigeau, G. de Loubens, and O. Klein
Author(s): T. Valet, K. Yamamoto, B. Pigeau, G. de Loubens, and O. Klein In the context of an ever-expanding experimental and theoretical interest in the magnetization dynamics of mesoscopic magnetic structures, both in the classical and quantum regimes, we formulate a low-energy field theory for the linear spin waves in finite and textured ferromagnets and we perform it… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 104437] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: High-Fidelity Control of a ¹³C Nuclear Spin Coupled to a Tin-Vacancy Center in Diamond
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. X
👤 Jeremias Resch, Ioannis Karapatzakis, Mohamed Elshorbagy, Marcel Schrodin, Philipp Fuchs, Philipp Graßhoff, Luis Kussi, Christoph Sürgers, Cyril Popov, Christoph Becher, Wolfgang Wernsdorfer, and David Hunger
Author(s): Jeremias Resch, Ioannis Karapatzakis, Mohamed Elshorbagy, Marcel Schrodin, Philipp Fuchs, Philipp Graßhoff, Luis Kussi, Christoph Sürgers, Cyril Popov, Christoph Becher, Wolfgang Wernsdorfer, and David Hunger High-fidelity control of a 13 C nuclear spin coupled to a tin-vacancy center yields coherence times exceeding 1.35 s using a superconducting waveguide, highlighting the potential of the tin-vacancy center as a coherent spin-photon interface for future quantum network applications. [Phys. Rev. X 16, 011060] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Light-enhanced dipolar interactions between exciton polaritons
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Yasufumi Nakano, Olivier Bleu, Brendan C. Mulkerin, Jesper Levinsen, and Meera M. Parish
Author(s): Yasufumi Nakano, Olivier Bleu, Brendan C. Mulkerin, Jesper Levinsen, and Meera M. Parish We consider the scenario of excitons in a semiconductor bilayer that are strongly coupled to cavity photons, leading to the formation of dipolar exciton polaritons (dipolaritons). Using a realistic pseudopotential for the dipolar interactions, we exactly determine the scattering between dipolaritons… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L121302] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Near room temperature magnetoelectric response and tunable magnetic anisotropy in the two-dimensional magnet 1T−CrTe₂
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Fengping Li, Bheema Lingam Chittari, Chao Lei, and Jeil Jung
Author(s): Fengping Li, Bheema Lingam Chittari, Chao Lei, and Jeil Jung Magnets with controllable magnetization and high critical temperature are essential for practical spintronics devices, among which the two-dimensional 1T−CrTe₂ stands out because of its high experimental critical temperature up to ∼300phantomůle0.28em0exK d… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 094440] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
